title: Aspidogyne multifoliata
taxonomy: Aspidogyne multifoliata is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This orchid is part of the genus Aspidogyne, which consists of a small group of species native to tropical regions. The classification of A. multifoliata places it within the subfamily Orchidoideae. Aspidogyne multifoliata is a terrestrial orchid, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and growth habits. Its classification is further refined within the tribe Cranichideae.

intro: Aspidogyne multifoliata, also known as the multifoliolate Aspidogyne, is a species of orchid native to tropical regions. The health benefits of Aspidogyne multifoliata include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the management of various infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Aspidogyne multifoliata is often used in traditional medicine and is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, as well as being used in the production of extracts and capsules.
